title: Shinjis glowing red marble
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's glowing red marble             Quote Post by IkariRei » Thu May 27, 2010 2:23 pm Just as planned? Or should i have just landed in the "you watch too much eva" thread

[]: Re: Shinji's glowing red marble             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu May 27, 2010 2:29 pm IkariRei wrote: Just as planned? Or should i have just landed in the "you watch too much eva" thread

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu May 27, 2010 3:48 pm It's popped up in discussion before but the only relevant thread I could find is: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6322 Once, long ago, either here or at the animenation forums, there was a thread titled something along the lines of "Look Mom! I found your soul!" which essentially link the red marble with a soul and Shinji discovering that it's his mom inside the Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu May 27, 2010 5:32 pm I detect the hand of JJ Abrams in this. (Him and his red spheres...)

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu May 27, 2010 10:45 pm Interesting... Never noticed that before.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat May 29, 2010 10:17 pm Yeah, an interesting "discovery", though I seemed to recall once thinking when I was watching it that "Hey, Shinji's holding the Red Earth!" I guess it didn't stick in my mind very long, though.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat May 29, 2010 11:36 pm Sharaz Destler wrote: I detect the hand of JJ Abrams in this. (Him and his lens flares...)

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat May 29, 2010 11:48 pm this is cool.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat May 29, 2010 11:51 pm I always felt that this cherry (I think it's a cherry or some other kind of berry), represented Shinji's soul, and that he was somehow offering it to Yui for some reason; either to somehow power Unit-01 or else simply as a metaphor for handing over control. This brings to mind something that occurred to me while going back over the old commentary pages. During episode #02, just before Unit-01 goes berserk, Maya mentions something about the synchrograph pulses flowing backwards . Now, this is accompanied by a seemingly desynchronising graph, but lets take what Maya says at face value for the present. Given that the synch pulse seemingly have direction, with the normal direction presumably meaning the Pilot controlling the Eva, could we infer the pulses flowing backwards to mean that the Eva is now controlling the pilot ? Now, this raises the question of what the Eva might need to control the pilot for. I would suggest that since the Evangelion is a cybernetic being, some level of control from the pilot is either required or useful for prolonged movement. Also of note is that the Eva's body—which is to be moved—is slightly distinct from the Eva's core, which would be doing the moving. But, would the berserk Eva really need or want to use the pilot to control .... the Eva? Well, in episode #16, shortly before the "marble" sequence, Shinji is seen curled up in the plug, ready to pass out. [wkimg]File:16 Shinji curled plug.jpg[/wkimg] But after Yui's visitation, and after Unit-01 has gone berserk, Shinji is seen waking up in a seated position in the plug, with his hands seemingly resting on the induction levers. [wkimg]File:16 Shinji Misato plug.jpg[/wkimg] Perhaps it's only a minor detail, but I would tentatively suggest it—along with the episode #02 dialogue—as evidence that the Eva(i.e. Yui) may be taking control of the pilot (Shinji) during the "berserk" sequences. This may be a) a simple by-product of the Eva taking over, b) useful or required to better controlling the cybernetic Eva, or c) an inherant side effect of the core—pilot system the Evangelions, which effectively gives one entity control over two.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sun May 30, 2010 2:15 am interesting to find something new shows how much detail was put into the show that is ripe for discovery

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun May 30, 2010 2:22 am i wanted to say that you're going out on a limb with the synchro graph pulse stuff, but it really doesn't make sense that the pilot would desychronize prior to berserk mode. I know this is getting off topic from the red sphere deal, but in episode 1-2, i recall it being stated that they've lost all readings on the pilot. We also have the technicolor shinji-face zoom in shot. Combined with the chatter about plug depth and pilot contamination, it seems more plausible that control could go both ways. is it possible Shinji was briefly tanged inside the plug? If we accept that this is what's happening in berserk mode (not the tanged part), then suddenly an old theory of Reichu's makes more sense (i think it was reichu) . It was something about unit 02 being berserk during the EoE harpy fight, while Asuka's just screaming along for the ride. Not that I'm endorsing this theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Jun 02, 2010 11:40 am BrikHaus wrote: fixed

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 02, 2010 12:41 pm I've thought exactly that for a long time, OMF, but never put it down in writing so coherently. Nice work!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 02, 2010 12:50 pm IkariRei: I don't think anyone has juxtaposed those two scenes in exactly that manner. Nice. ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I always felt that this cherry (I think it's a cherry or some other kind of berry)
